programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse's quiet fishing trip with his estranged father takes an unexpected turn thanks to revelations about international espionage. Drama, guest starring Patrick Macnee, Robert Vaughn, Phil Morris and Robert Culp.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Mafia Priest Mystery - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The crime-busting cleric finds himself caught up in a strange mystery as he attempts to prove a fellow priest innocent of murder. But with the real killer now dead, he must try to establish a connection with the Mafia. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Credulous Quarry
programme.desc: Perry finds himself tied up in a perplexing case when he clears his client from a hit-and-run homicide, only to have him charged with first degree murder.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker helps a man he believes has been framed for murder. Chuck Norris stars.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man is arrested for shooting his wife, with whom he was engaged in a bitter custody battle for their eight-year-old daughter, in front of eyewitnesses - but it later transpires that she was hit by bullets from a second gun. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Face in the Mirror Mystery
programme.desc: The crime-solving clergyman's evil half-brother appears on the scene, with his eye on some precious gems. Unfortunately, the police are unable to tell the pair apart. Drama, starring Tom Bosley, Tracy Nelson and Richard Beale.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Ill-Fated Faker
programme.desc: Perry is asked by a harassed businessman to help him get rid of a freeloading nephew, who - unbeknownst to him - is also having an affair with his wife.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.sub-title: Something in the Shadows - Part One
programme.desc: A drugs ring is investigated, and a student is raped on campus, prompting the lawman to suspect a serial sex offender is on the lose.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo.(n)

programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan.(n)

programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: At the End of a Long Day
programme.desc: Yvonne sets off for Barton Street Station to face formal manslaughter charges but has a run-in with a drunk en route and ends up hitting him - which won't exactly count in her favour when she appears in court. Jo Masters discovers one of Phil's informants is dealing in drugs with the collusion of a crooked customs officer, while Roger and Dan find a missing schoolgirl in woodlands and realise she has just given birth - but can't see any sign of her baby.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Elliot's wife needs treatment when her tracheotomy becomes infected, but when Connie tries to help she finds there is no record of the previous operation in Gina's notes. Kyla's former husband visits the hospital, determined to spend time with their son, and one of Sam's patients puts Marcia in an awkward position.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Barney faces a life-or-death dilemma, Penny's medical skills are tested to the limit during a diving course, and Holly is mystified at the collapse of a promising teenage dancer. Medical drama, starring Donna Alexander, Ronnie McCann and Una Stubbs.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Acting Superintendent Amanda Prosser arrives at Sun Hill, where she makes a good impression on Jo Masters and Phil Hunter. Zain discovers Smithy is sleeping with Louise Larson, and warns him the consequences could be deadly if her husband finds out. June is forced to deal with a date-rape case on her first day back at work, while Yvonne resolves to admit she assaulted a drunk the night before - but reckons without Honey and Tony, who talk her out of it and destroy CCTV evidence of the attack.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: There's a rift in the family dynamics between Meena, Sanjay & Gita after the death of their mother and Arthur makes a big decision about his life in the square.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Beth returns to Nigel, but will he forgive and forget or is his broken heart damaged beyond repair and Michelle's secret is out, thanks to Mandy, how will everyone react?(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Bayleaf reassesses his future with the force, and Nick is pressured into making a tough decision. The brigade are called to help a man is being held hostage in a car.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Donald and Ewan's fishing trip inadvertently brings a long-buried secret to light with the discovery of a lost German fighter plane in the loch. Isobel knows her godfather, local butcher Irvine Taylor, was the pilot and resolves to keep everyone else in the dark - but an MoD investigation threatens to scupper her plans. Elsewhere, Molly is devastated to learn she has a heart condition and a lonely Jess unwittingly agrees to take part in a karaoke competition with Ewan.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The new head of the primary school upsets Beth by suggesting that a much-loved teacher's erratic behaviour may be down to a drinking problem, but when pupils are put at risk the nurse realises there could be an even more serious explanation. Meanwhile, the Scorpions face a difficult match and Anna fears she's jeopardised the factory's future when she gives an unhelpful bank manager a piece of her mind.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey spreads her wings for the first time since her husband was put behind bars, and invites a handsome footballer round for dinner. Meanwhile, Sharon discovers the joys of literature. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: The local mayor becomes embroiled in a sex scandal, so Boycie decides to stand for election as his replacement - unaware Llewellyn is planning to do the same. Marlene is put in a tight spot when a schoolfriend of Tyler's confesses he has developed feelings for her. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Patrick searches for his father, while Stephen aspires to be a celebrity.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy feels the need to do something special for the Queen's Silver Jubilee so he volunteers himself, Clegg, and Compo to help with the town's pageant. However, the need arises to find someone capable of driving a tractor. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo are called to an unusual crime scene, where archaeologists searching for an ancient burial site are in conflict with builders working on a bypass. The investigation suffers a setback when a fight between the two factions destroys all the evidence. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra reopens an investigation into the suspected suicide of a Foreign Office diplomat, whose body was found in a frozen London lake after she had suffered the personal tragedy of a miscarriage and the professional embarrassment of having a government laptop stolen from her home. The woman's fiance and twin sister both believe her death is connected to sensitive information held on the missing computer and blame her boss for her downfall - and when Sandra clashes with the man himself, it only makes her more determined to pursue the conspiracy theory, despite Strickland's advice to tread carefully. Simon Day and Sharon Small guest star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Forensic pathologist Dr Sam Ryan discovers evidence of physical violence on the body of a girl who supposedly drowned accidentally. Once the child's disturbing family history is known, headstrong Ryan defies police chiefs and sets out on a murder investigation. Drama, starring Amanda Burton, with Lesley Dunlop and Carl Hillsden.(n)

Mark discovers he has a hereditary condition, leaving him in an awkward position with Chrissie, and Kyla worries Max is gay when she finds a love letter he has written.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Max returns to work for the first time since son Frank's death and is determined to prove he's made a full recovery. However, his reaction to traumatic events during his shift makes it clear he has a long way to go yet. Meanwhile, a stripper's frisky snakes are proving less than charming to the A&E staff. Medical drama, starring Robert Gwilym and Ian Bleasdale.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As her trial nears, Yvonne learns the drunk she recently assaulted has been called to give evidence against her, and tearfully tells Tony she is sure the prosecution have enough evidence to put her away for a long time. Amber is paired up with Honey and sent to find the man responsible for two date-rape incidents, but puts herself at risk trying to catch the suspect and almost becomes his latest victim. Phil beats up Steve for sleeping with Kate Maltby, while Zain introduces Smithy to an informant who has had two fingers chopped off by Pete Larson, and warns he runs the risk of losing a more intimate part of his anatomy if he's not careful.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian confronts Kathy about vicious rumours, but finds the truth hard to take. Mrs Hewitt tries to find Arthur but runs foul of Mark and Nigel looks ready to play happy families.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel's gone missing, but only Phil seems to care. Meanwhile, he and Sanjay have to decide what to do with the dogs. There's a new addition at the Butchers'.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A motorist has a heart attack behind the wheel, and the resulting smash-up sees Nick risking his life to save a man trapped in a phone booth by a blazing car.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: After an administrative error jeopardises the award of a vital grant, Paul agrees to give former Army officer Alex a greater role in managing the estate, despite having reservations about his leadership style. Jess decides to play matchmaker between Golly and Glenbogle's new dance teacher, Meg, and Donald's plans to throw a wild stag party for one of his old racing pals are shattered when his friend reveals he simply wants a quiet weekend away.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Anna faces every parent's worst nightmare as baby Amy is rushed to hospital with signs of meningitis and when the diagnosis is confirmed, panic sets in among the townsfolk. Anna keeps vigil by her daughter's side, while Luke plays truant from school, sending Karen on a desperate mission to find him. Beth and Tom give in to their feelings for each other.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1989. The Essex girls steel themselves for a lonely Christmas away from their jailbird husbands. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie decides his staff need to work harder and vows to sack the next employee he catches slacking. Mrs Cakeworthy is the first out the door, but she soon has her boss regretting his decision when he learns she is a witch. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Ella Kenion star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel wants peace and quiet, but ends up in hospital along with Patrick, Jean and Stephen.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg attends the funeral of an old friend, and later tries to convince Foggy and Compo that flowers have emotions and that they react to vocal tone and music. Compo's attention is more focused on his own needs, in particular enacting a new plan to drive Nora Batty into his arms. Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brain Wilde star.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The maverick judge is exiled to Warwick by his fellow judges, who feel he must be disciplined for his indiscretion with a claimant. Meanwhile, Jo hears the case of a PA accused of embezzling millions from his boss, the head of a high-ranking electronics corporation - and the trial grows heated when allegations are made about a government minister taking bribes. Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired detective Steve McAndrew arrives from Glasgow to help reinvestigate one of his original cases when a missing girl's DNA is found at the scene of a petrol station robbery in Clapham, south London. It's clear that the inquiry is a very personal one for McAndrew, and although his personality rubs the team up the wrong way, his methods soon pay dividends. Denis Lawson stars.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As her trial nears, Yvonne learns the drunk she recently assaulted has been called to give evidence against her, and tearfully tells Tony she is sure the prosecution have enough evidence to put her away for a long time. Amber is paired up with Honey and sent to find the man responsible for two date-rape incidents, but puts herself at risk trying to catch the suspect and almost becomes his latest victim. Phil beats up Steve for sleeping with Kate Maltby, while Zain introduces Smithy to an informant who has had two fingers chopped off by Pete Larson, and warns he runs the risk of losing a more intimate part of his anatomy if he's not careful.(n)

Medical drama, starring Ian Bleasdale, Cathy Shipton and Claire Goose.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack takes a break from paperwork to help investigate a case alongside CID officers, only to discover his increasingly wayward son Ben may be the mastermind behind a major-league drugs operation. Yvonne's trial gets under way, and Laura Bryant begins work as a PC, while a sceptical Sheelagh kidnaps a youngster allegedly suffering from leukaemia, unwilling to believe there's anything wrong with her.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is

Drama, starring Kerrie Taylor and Julian Lewis-Jones.(n)

programme.desc: Beth and Tyler announce they have become vegetarians and set off to Ludlow races to protest about the treatment of animals. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.desc: Compo and Clegg gatecrash Foggy's trip to see a female friend in Wales. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen.(n)

programme.desc: A young woman's body is found in a seemingly idyllic Northumberland coastal village in 1966, and the subsequent investigation leads Gently and Bacchus to suspect the victim's estranged husband is responsible for the killing. However, they soon come to realise her disturbed family is hiding an even more shocking secret. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? programme.title: Nadiya: Anxiety and Me
programme.desc: Since Nadiya Hussain won The Great British Bake Off in 2015, she's rarely been off TV screens. But behind the scenes Nadiya suffers from extreme anxiety and debilitating panic attacks, which she's had since childhood. For decades, she has kept her anxiety a secret, ashamed to speak out. Now, she sets out to find the cause of her anxiety, exploring the most effective, available treatments, while also having therapy herself, in the hope of managing her anxiety.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final stage of the culinary contest begins, with the four remaining contestants embarking on a culinary adventure of a lifetime on the island of Mauritius, where they will face three extraordinary challenges. The first challenge takes place in the town of Mahebourg, one of the island's earliest established fishing ports. Split into teams of two, each pair will be tasked with cooking two traditional Mauritian street food dishes for local fishermen. Next, the amateurs travel to the island's capital, Port Louis, where they have the honour of working at the Labourdonnais hotel during a busy lunch service. Finally, the contestants face the daunting challenge of creating a dinner for mentors Chef Moroogun Coopen and Chef Peeroo Nizam, as well as some of the leading figures in Mauritius' food and cultural community.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the Grand Final as six contenders return to the black chair to decide which of them will be crowned champion. They answer questions of the specialist subjects of the Stonewall Riots, the ancient universities of Scotland, the Wars of the Roses, the No 1 Ladies' Detective Agency books, Johannes Vermeer, and the films of Kevin Smith, before having two minutes each to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry and doctor and broadcaster Michael Mosley join an Imperial College, London, experiment to assess the brainpower of the nation, putting members of the public to the test in a battle of wits as they pit young and old, males and females, and tech lovers and readers against one other. The results reveal new science about how people's intelligence changes through their lives, while Hannah and Michael examine groundbreaking new science on how lifestyle and love of technology is changing the human brain.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of the quiz, with Sandi Toksvig inviting Nish Kumar, David Mitchell and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions about quads and quins. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The $50m Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: With the country still in lockdown, Matt Baker is on his home patch, building a pond, meeting a peregrine that lacks the confidence to hunt, and getting crafty with the paintbrushes in his back garden. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks reveals the world beneath the water of his garden pond, Adam Henson has a lot a lot of hungry mouths to feed on his farm, and Kate Humble takes viewers round her home village to find out how the community is pulling together in these challenging times.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon explores the eastern Mediterranean, beginning in Cyprus, where he goes on patrol with UN peacekeepers overseeing an abandoned buffer zone. He moves on to Lebanon, where he discovers the roots of Christianity, and Israel, where he boards a navy vessel guarding the country's offshore oil reserves. Finally, he crosses the world's most heavily fortified borders into Gaza, where he meets a woman helping reconstruction efforts by making bricks into ash.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Gloucester Antiques Centre, where the red and blue teams go shopping for treasures with experts Kate Bliss and Charles Hanson. Away from the contest, Charlie learns about Glevum Games, a local company which was at the top of its game at the start of the 20th century.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers try out a smell-proof dog bag, a hand cream designed especially for nurses and a pirate-themed board game featuring 100 original characters.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, craftsman David Burville takes on the repair of a little clockwork tractor that holds significant memories for owner Nicola Clemo, as it belonged to her father. Meanwhile, Sandy Richards from Cornwall arrives, bearing a precious but delicate memento of her beloved parents for the attention of ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay, a plate bearing a photograph of Sandy's mother. Elsewhere, a military compass and an old vibraphone are restored by experts Barry Phillips and Pete Woods.(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Singer Alfie Boe discovers how his grandfather helped protect the nation during the war, as a member of the Home Guard later immortalised in Dad's Army.(n)

programme.title: Reach for the Sky (1956)
programme.desc: Biopic of Second World War hero Douglas Bader. A plane crash in 1931 cost the aspiring pilot both his legs, but after learning to walk on artificial limbs, he went on to become one of the RAF's most decorated heroes. However, more misfortune was in store when he was shot down over occupied France and taken prisoner. Starring Kenneth More, Muriel Pavlow and Alexander Knox.(n)

programme.title: Churchill
programme.sub-title: Churchill: To Conquer or to Die
programme.desc: Second in a four-part series chronicling the life of Britain's great wartime leader, examining how Churchill became the symbol of the country's struggle for survival through the crises of Dunkirk, the Battle of Britain and the Blitz.(n)

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott and Strictly Come Dancing judge Len Goodman arrive in Bristol, where Len has his first taste of Jamaican food and samples some Spanish cold soup. Meanwhile, Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a dish inspired by his heritage.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Christina Trevanion value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, while Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero Dylan Thomas.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards test their general knowledge skills in more trivia-based games, with the winner announced on Friday.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers head to Ryedale to show off the finest animals in their flocks, including a retired businessman who is hoping to carry on his wife's legacy.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in Essex that produces 400,000 cereal bars a day, and follows the production process from the arrival of the ingredients to the eventual dispatch, discovering the importance of getting the perfect balance between honey and glucose. Cherry Healey travels to South Africa to help with the macadamia harvest, where a quarter of the world's supply of these nuts are farmed. Historian Ruth Goodman climbs a mountain in the Lake District to investigate the history of one of the oldest forms of snack bar - the Kendal Mint Cake.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Nigel Havers, Alex Brooker, Sally Lindsay, Georgia Toffolo and Desiree Burch play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: The Day California Burned
programme.desc: Documentary examining the devastating wildfire that swept through northern California in November 2018, causing the deaths of more than 80 people and approximately $16.5billion of damage. The film features interviews with firefighters, police officers and local residents who came face to face with the fire, including a nurse who evacuated her patients before finding herself trapped in the middle of the blaze.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: For the first time ever, the Beechgrove presenters give weekly updates from their own patches of Scotland, beginning with Carole Baxter in Aberdeenshire.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex and visits a unique collection at the Millennium Seed Bank, while David Harper and Raj Bisram help the teams.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a specially designed pram for messy eating, a super-absorbent sponge that can soak up 12 times as much water as normal, and an anti-theft sports bag.(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Julian Peedle-Calloo and Yvonnne Cobb present from their homes, learning more about the history of video calling and how Covid-19 has affected the deaf community. With voiceover.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A young boy has to take control of the car when his mother has a seizure behind the wheel. Elsewhere, an off-duty fireman helps a woman who has fallen into a crater in Bali.(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Tanni Grey-Thompson reflects on her grandfather's experiences during the First and Second World War, including his time working as an air raid warden.(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.sub-title: Eric Knowles vs Ochuko Ojiri Foreign Antiques Market
programme.desc: Eric Knowles goes head to head with Ochuko Ojiri in Villeneuve Les Avignon. Ochuko is on the hunt for retro chic, while Eric seeks classic French antiques to bring back to the UK.(n)

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg visits Cliveden House in Berkshire, where he explores how the upper classes lived during the 1920s, revealing the tragic story of Britain's original 'It' girl Elizabeth Ponsonby. He also discovers what life was like for the home's head butler Edwin Lee.(n)

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: Martin Gilbert focuses on Winston Churchill's policies from 1943 to the end of the war, a crucial period that saw the Allied invasion of Sicily, D-Day and his second Moscow visit. Always keen to meddle in military strategy, he also engaged in highly sensitive negotiations with the Russians - until forced to acknowledge Britain's economic and military limitations.(n)

programme.title: The Desert Fox (1951)
programme.desc: German commander Rommel falls out of favour with Hitler following his defeat at El Alamein. On returning to his homeland, he is torn between his oath of loyalty and lack of faith in the Fuehrer's unstable leadership, prompting him to become involved in an assassination plot. Second World War drama, starring James Mason, Jessica Tandy, Cedric Hardwicke, Leo G Carroll and Luther Adler.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len has his first taste of Middle Eastern food and samples Peruvian ceviche in central London. Later, Ainsley takes inspiration from the day and heads to the kitchen to cook chicken shawarma.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Davies are at Huddersfield Town Hall to find items to take to auction, while Paul Martin finds out about former prime minister Harold Wilson.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards continue to test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: In Bath, Hugh tries to sell his family's award winning cheese, and the Kimber family will be selling their meat and dairy products straight off the farm.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Ruth Hansom, Alex Bond, Samira Effa and Josh Angus compete in the heats for the North East. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature. This week, the chefs are specifically tasked with honouring the theme of realism in children's literature, as well as heralding books and authors from their region. Dishes include a Secret Garden-inspired starter of potatoes, alliums, egg and caviar, and a Railway Children-inspired creation comprising traditional North East delicacies.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Determined to derail destiny, Lily comes up with a plan - ignore the prediction that she is due at Devs. Meanwhile, Lyndon yearns to return to Devs, but first he must persuade Katie. Later, an unsettled Stewart spouts verse when uncultured Forest encounters him at the entrance to Devs, and as times ticks down to the fixed point Katie told Lily of, Forest seeks solace deep in the past.(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Filmed before the coronavirus crisis swept the nation, the comedian takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, working as a carer. As well as facing the challenge of doing intimate personal care, Rhod is nearly drowned by an octogenarian in a swimming pool, and finds himself upstaged for bawdy humour by a poet with Parkinsons.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that has - and hasn't - happened in recent news.(n)

programme.title: Horizon

The results reveal new science about how people's intelligence changes through their lives, while Hannah and Michael examine groundbreaking new science on how lifestyle and love of technology is changing the human brain.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cool blue waterway winding effortlessly from the far away high peaks and evergreens in a mountain landscape.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Bones
programme.desc: Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod explores how having a skeleton has helped vertebrates to colonise and dominate almost every habitat on Earth. He begins his journey by looking at the influence of bones on size, revealing how they are capable of supporting large animals, with the strongest bone able to withstand 109 tons of pressure.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Romans with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The professor delves deeper into ordinary Roman life by going behind the closed doors of the family home. She tells the stories of eccentric wife Glyconis - praised by her husband for liking a drink or two - Allia Potestas, who lived in a menage a trois, and 11-year-old schoolboy Sulpicius Maximus, worked to death by his pushy parents. Mary also paints a more detailed picture of slavery, revealing that many Romans chose to be buried with their servants.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True History
programme.desc: The Private Eye editor explores the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years, investigating how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Sam Willis concludes his exploration of the criminal underworld of the 17th and 18th centuries by looking at urban crime, fraud and corruption, uncovering a fascinating rogues' gallery of villains. He examines the case of thief and serial escaper Jack Sheppard, who was so notorious that almost a quarter of a million people turned up to witness his hanging, while fraudster Mary Toft managed to convince George I's surgeon that she had given birth to rabbits.(n)

programme.title: Elegance and Decadence: The Age of the Regency
programme.sub-title: Warts and All - Portrait of a Prince
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores how British culture was transformed between 1811 and 1820, the period known as the Regency era. She begins by profiling George, the Prince Regent, and charts how his rivalry with Napoleon served as a catalyst for change and led to the development of a grandiose architectural style that changed the face of London.(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore describes how the Habsburgs transformed Vienna into a multinational city of music, culture, art and ideas. Simon explores the Habsburgs' rise to power and discovers more about how Vienna marked Europe's front line in the struggle to defend both Christendom and the Catholic Church from revolutionaries who plotted to destroy them.(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.desc: Professor Richard Clay asks whether utopia is a state of mind that can therefore be found within. He explores the many means people have created to immerse themselves in a perfect moment, of epiphany or transcendence, pushing the boundaries of artistic expression and pleasure. Seeking answers in a broad range of arts, the art historian tries to compose a haiku and uncovers traces of the hedonistic medieval carnival tradition in the churches and pubs of Lancashire. He also compares and contrasts different musical escapes, interviewing Acid House legend A Guy Called Gerald (aka Gerald Simpson) and the celebrated minimalist composer Steve Reich.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cool blue waterway winding effortlessly from the far away high peaks and evergreens in a mountain landscape.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True History
programme.desc: The Private Eye editor explores the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years, investigating how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an old weathered cabin buried deep in the snow and lined with protective evergreens.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Bones
programme.desc: Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod explores how skeletons have adapted to allow vertebrates to move on land in different ways, such as swinging through trees, sliding along forest floors, and running across plains at speed. He looks at the spines of both cheetahs and snakes, and uncovers the role of the pentadactyl limb.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley's three-part documentary exploring the history of medicine concludes as he tells the story of how scientists went from finding antidotes to poisons to applying otherwise dangerous toxins as a cure. He reveals why he believes, of all the natural remedies featured in his scientific tour, the tales of treatments derived from deadly substances are perhaps the most extraordinary he encountered.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr on Churchill: Blood, Sweat and Oil Paint
programme.desc: The broadcaster investigates Winston Churchill's lifelong love for painting and reveals the ways in which his hobby helped shape his career as politician and statesman. Andrew travels to the south of France and Morocco to discovers how the former PM's serious approach to the craft of led to friendships with major British artists of the 20th century, and he finds out how a single painting may have influenced the course of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: The Culture Show
programme.sub-title: The Art of WWII: A Culture Show Special
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the often overlooked history of the wartime art boom, meetingBlitz survivors, factory workers and Land Girls who became the subject of paintings, talks to contemporary artists about the challenges of creating work in conflict zones and considers how the images produced during the 'People's War' laid the groundwork for a modern understanding of what art should be.(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank's Warsaw: Resurrecting History
programme.desc: Historian and TV presenter Dan Cruickshank returns to his childhood home of Warsaw for the first time in almost sixty years and explores his boyhood memories of the city and its people. Warsaw suffered destruction during the Second World War, but rose from the ashes in one of the greatest reconstruction jobs in history.(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the impact on the period of Venice, which was a meeting point between east and west. He looks at how the exciting colours of Bellini, the mystery of Giorgione, the brilliance of Titian, the drama of Tintoretto and the elegance of Veronese created a new set of Renaissance rules - and produced inventive and visually stunning art.(n)

programme.title: The Art That Made Mexico: Paradise, Power and Prayers
programme.desc: Alinka Echeverria explores how faith has always driven cultural life in Mexico, exploring spectacular art that blends Mesoamerican and Catholic iconography.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an old weathered cabin buried deep in the snow and lined with protective evergreens.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley's three-part documentary exploring the history of medicine concludes as he tells the story of how scientists went from finding antidotes to poisons to applying otherwise dangerous toxins as a cure. He reveals why he believes, of all the natural remedies featured in his scientific tour, the tales of treatments derived from deadly substances are perhaps the most extraordinary he encountered.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Bones
programme.desc: Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod uncovers the ways that the skeleton aids flight, looking at what makes the humble pigeon a skilled flier and how albatrosses manage to complete long migration journeys. He also explores why some birds have dense bones, and reveals which airborne animal he believes is the 'ultimate'.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.sub-title: The Great Famine and the Black Death
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the great famine and the Black Death affected Kibworth during the 14th century. Delving into the local archives and aided by on-site archaeological excavations, he learns not only that two-thirds of the village's population were lost, but also how these tragic events helped create community spirit.(n)

programme.title: Double Agent: The Eddie Chapman Story
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre revisits the story of wartime double agent Eddie Chapman, the subject matter of his best-selling book Agent Zigzag. He reveals how, during the Second World War, Chapman was able to dupe Nazi Germany so successfully that he was awarded the Iron Cross, the country's most prestigious decoration. Includes footage from an interview the former spy gave three years before his death in 1997.(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles concludes his series charting the history of archaeological breakthroughs by discovering more about the ancient common man. The investigation takes the archaeologist from the elaborate grave of Egyptian boy king Tutankhamun to the Danish resting place of the Tollund Man, who died as part of an Iron Age sacrifice more than 2,000 years ago.(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.sub-title: St Petersburg: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the art and architecture of the Russian city, taking in a selection of imperial, communist and modern-day treasures.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A yoga teacher and her partner have their hearts set on a water birth, but when they arrive at the hospital, they are confronted with an unforeseen obstacle to their plans. A 22-year-old woman whose pregnancy was unplanned needs to be induced, but the birth progresses very slowly. However, her mother and her boyfriend are both on hand to support her through the struggle. A woman who had part of her cervix removed while suffering from cancer prepares to give birth to her third child, and reveals how her new partner has become a vital presence in the lives of her two daughters.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Joe struggles to cope with the changes resulting from his parents' divorce, as he spends his days shuttling back and forth between the old house in the Lakes and his mother's new home in Manchester. Since retiring, Maurice has become a volunteer firefighter, and is called in to a fire at Paul's house. When he decides to show Joe the damage, it only leaves the youngster feeling more adrift and drives him to reject his music.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: At a fundraising event at the club, one of Connell's friends Karen does her best to show Marianne some kindness, but Rachel behaves coldly towards her, and the evening turns sour when one of the boys' older friends gropes Marianne. Connell becomes anxious when his friends tease him about Marianne, suspecting they've guessed about their relationship. Fuelled by anxiety, he makes an unexpected decision that hurts Marianne and causes a rift between him and his mother Lorraine. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is alarmed to discover Jean has not been taking her medication - so he goes to extreme lengths to ensure she has her tablets. Another setback leaves Linda more determined than ever to conquer her demons, Ian plans a surprise for Sharon, and Bobby asks Dotty out on a date. Denise gives Isaac some home truths.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A yoga teacher and her partner have their hearts set on a water birth, but when they arrive at the hospital, they are confronted with an unforeseen obstacle to their plans. A 22-year-old woman whose pregnancy was unplanned needs to be induced, but the birth progresses very slowly. However, her mother and her boyfriend are both on hand to support her through the struggle. A woman who had part of her cervix removed while suffering from cancer prepares to give birth to her third child, and reveals how her new partner has become a vital presence in the lives of her two daughters.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Joe struggles to cope with the changes resulting from his parents' divorce, as he spends his days shuttling back and forth between the old house in the Lakes and his mother's new home in Manchester. Since retiring, Maurice has become a volunteer firefighter, and is called in to a fire at Paul's house. When he decides to show Joe the damage, it only leaves the youngster feeling more adrift and drives him to reject his music.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: More family heirlooms are restored, including an antique skeleton clock, a giant teddy bear and a transistor radio with huge sentimental value to its owner.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The ten remaining home sewers return to the nation's most famous sewing room for children's week, and are asked to make a classic smocked dress in the pattern challenge. The task requires the sewers to combine tricky techniques, including making their own bias binding, and a Peter Pan collar. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers let their imaginations run wild, turning sleeping bags into food shaped fancy dress. Finally, the contestants are asked to make a pair of dungarees for a little boy or girl.(n)

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.sub-title: Global Questions: Coronavirus Crisis: Our Economy
programme.desc: As the Coronavirus takes hold around the world, Global Questions invites its international audience to put their questions to a panel of experts on the impact on the Global Economy. With dire warning of the worst recession since the 1930's what will the fallout be? Are we mortgaging the future of the young for the sake of the old and will we see a return to big government intervention economics? Join Zeinab Badawi and two leading international figures answering your questions from around the world.(n)

programme.desc: Starting in south Skye, sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee catch the Kylerhea ferry over to Glenelg before taking on a steep cycle climb up Mam Ratagan and finishing in Shiel Bridge.(n)

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil - An Ceòl (The Musical Path - The Music)

Plus, Lau one of Scotland's most respected traditional bands.(n)

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: With the Scottish National competition complete, a look at the International competition, hosted in Northern Ireland at the Gill Hall Estate in Co Down. Among the competitors are Billy Common from Scotland and Thomas Longton from England. Plus, some information about the location and the prize cattle reared on its fertile land.(n)

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon visits St Andrews where he attended university. Recreating an old student tradition, he plunges into the freezing cold North Sea, feeds penguins at the aquarium and explores the hidden depths of a Cold War bunker.(n)

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom

programme.title: A'Bhuth (Early Stages Mathematics)

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a new garden for an eight-year-old girl in Nottingham, despite the worst weather they have ever experienced. Tilly Sawford was severely scalded in a freak accident six years ago and continues to require treatment to aid her recovery, leading to the staff at her local hospital recommending the family have a safe garden created for them, with a bold colour scheme and surprise areas for all of the children.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Alone, heavily pregnant and fed up with life, Mel faces having to go through the birth of her first child without the married father of her baby, who has chosen to stay with his wife and family. programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David Collins is unexpectedly acquitted on a technicality having served seven years of a life sentence for the murder of his wife Tara. Priya is disappointed that she has to attend her father's party over spending time with Al, and Belle frets at the thought of Jamie going on holiday with Andrea.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An exhausted Yasmeen sits in her police cell while the police search No 6 for evidence, unaware that they are being filmed on Geoff's CCTV. Alya returns from Spain and heads straight to the police station, Gary leaps to Kelly's defence when Dev blames her for heaping so much trouble on Asha, and Adam and Sarah return from their honeymoon.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on a journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the second episode, Ryan, Adam and Scott attempt to find their inner zen at a huge meditation hall, and Scott gets a fiery fix-up Indian style by a hairdresser who prefers flames to scissors. Later, a special family meeting leads to showman Dougie hitting the stage in Mumbai.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a 10-week-old collie who has been rejected by its mother, and helps to find a home for a mongrel called Hope who has been at Battersea for over 300 days. Elsewhere, Paul has got his work cut out when a family of nine whippets arrive on site, including some three-week-old puppies in need of some love.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Four short dramas depicting life in lockdown. The worst fears of paranoid, self-absorbed hypochondriac Mike have come true. There is an unstoppable virus that affects us all, and he can catch it just by breathing the same air as other people. As he struggles to cope with his fears during the Covid-19 outbreak, he insists on an online session with his old friend and therapist, Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: Alice and Rob's relationship continues to disintegrate as evidence under questioning incriminates them further and the children grow closer to David. programme.desc: Peter's past as a sperm donor catches up with him when several of his offspring unexpectedly show up at his house.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter loses his job at the Happy-Go-Lucky toy factory, but the blow is cushioned by an unexpected windfall in the form of a massive welfare payout. First-ever episode of the animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane, Alex Borstein and Seth Green.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys buy an eccentric slave called Mushki at an auction and Grumio finds his position in the household gradually being usurped by the newcomer. Marcus is impressed with his new servant, but Stylax has a sneaking suspicion that Mushki might be a psychopath. Comedy, starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry and Ryan Sampson, with Tim Key and David Schneider.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: It's a public holiday in Rome for the local elections, so Marcus and Stylax sign up as hired clappers for wig-wearing politician Victor of the Gracchii. However, Grumio is drawn toward another candidate named Julius Priscus - but it could simply be because he is giving away free promotional plums. Comedy, guest starring Simon Callow and Jonny Sweet.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is stunned when she hears that it's Steve and Vicky's wedding day. Alec and Bet turn up at the registry office intent on stopping the wedding.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky assures Steve she can handle Alec. Ken tells Denise he wants to buy number one. Fiona very publicly gives Steve's unpaid bills to Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak misunderstands the tourist board's response to their inspection. Rachel accepts Chris's marriage proposal. Terry suggests setting up a youth club for the village youngsters.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Hislops spend an awful night at the Dingles' home. Caroline feels guilty leaving Kathy to cope with Nick and Alice. Terry has second thoughts about helping Viv with the disco.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The villagers fear for a boy's life when he disappears while exploring the gardens of a derelict house with his brother and sister. Wetherby learns he has fallen down an old well and a media circus descends on the scene as firemen begin their rescue attempt. However, panic ensues when shots are fired from the house. Meanwhile, a troublesome celebrity journalist goes in search of local gossip.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Oh! To Be in England - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff assures Dave that he's not going to take advantage of Linda. The village disco ends in embarrassment for Butch. Biff catches Dave and Kim together.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers worry when Linda doesn't come home. Chris and Rachel's wedding day arrives. Frank takes some drastic measures and disowns Chris.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia keeps Jamie off school to guard the house. Cliff's solicitor asks Jack for a meeting. Tracy rows with Deirdre for cutting her off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise wishes Deirdre would move away so she and Ken can get on with their lives. Josie is attacked while locking up the surgery. The Duckworths receive some stunning news.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Cheap Flat
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse is assigned to protect an informant who is undergoing emergency surgery. The case brings back unpleasant memories for the detective, while his colleagues are thrown into disarray when Bright is also rushed to hospital. Morse becomes fixated with a series of mysterious deaths that have occurred on the ward, and tries to find the scientific explanation behind stories of a curse.(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Oh! To Be in England - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson try to trace Rosie's whereabouts in Australia, and Mick becomes a suspect when they uncover evidence of domestic violence in his relationship with her. Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is still fuming about Chris getting married. Kim tells Frank that she is pregnant. Frank overhears Dave and Biff talking about Dave's affair with Kim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The doctor tells Kim that Frank has suffered liver failure. Sam invites Eric to spend Christmas Day with the Dingles. Chris apologises to his unconscious father in hospital.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie can't take her mind off her ordeal. Jack is full of big plans on how to spend the money. Mike offers Tricia one of his flats in Crimea Street.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona decides to move on from Steve. Ken helps Tricia move into Crimea Street so he can move into number one. Alec refuses to pass on Steve's messages to Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson try to trace Rosie's whereabouts in Australia, and Mick becomes a suspect when they uncover evidence of domestic violence in his relationship with her. Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Western Star
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: Vivien shocks her children by announcing she plans to sell their childhood home and go travelling with Mark. Each of the three siblings also has personal problems of their own to contend with - Helen's actions at work are beginning to have an impact on her marriage, Jake is shocked to discover that Leila might be moving on, and Natalie's affair starts to threaten her physical safety.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Stranger at the Gates - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A handsome man from Denmark arrives at the household in search of work, setting Mariette's heart racing with his dashing good looks, but Charley is not impressed by the visitor's charms. Drama, starring David Jason, Catherine Zeta-Jones and Philip Franks.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Dave spend the night together in Frank's bed. Eric struggles to get into the Christmas spirit. Kathy confronts Dave about who he spent the night with.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave shocks Biff by telling him he loves Kim. Zak's brother Albert turns up at the Dingles. Kim tells Kathy that Dave is the father of her baby.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack and Vera try to sell Cliff's belongings. Alec explodes when Steve asks for his blessing to marry Vicky. Alma doesn't like the idea of Deirdre being Mike's caretaker.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky books the wedding despite Alec's objections. Denise and Ken move into number one. Rita takes Sally and the children on holiday. Don starts divorce proceedings.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: The children's ongoing distrust starts to put a strain on Vivien and Mark's relationship. Meanwhile, Natalie struggles deal with her situation with Tony, and Jake turns to Stella for advice. Later, Jake unearths a darkly disturbing secret about Mark's past. Starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is startled to see the scale of the drug order he is being told to courier and even more shocked when he realises Billy and Cain are being used as decoys to draw police attention away from him. Chas is shocked at how much Paddy has been struggling with Eve, and Andrea struggles to keep a cool demeanour in front of Jamie and Millie.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)
