programme.desc: Howard has a meeting with Colonel Williams, a representative from the Air Force Department of Material Command, about the gyroscope he invented with Leonard and Sheldon. Penny accidentally reveals that Bernadette is pregnant to their colleagues at work. Dean Norris guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tensions rise when the guys struggle to complete their Air Force project on time and are forced to work around the clock - leaving Sheldon fighting to stay awake. Penny and Amy attend a university gathering at the home of Bert the geologist. Raj learns Bernadette's true feelings about her pregnancy when he helps her to clear out the spare room for a nursery.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Soon-to-be parents Iman and Heather Ahmed want a vintage-inspired nursery for their baby, but Heather has never used a sewing machine. Can Kirstie help them create the bedroom that they dream of for their first child?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Graham Hartridge and Maggie Rogers have been together for 18 months and fancy an adventure. He would love to buy a place in the south-west of France, but she is nervous of taking the plunge and would prefer Devon - much closer to family and friends. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help with their property search.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are on the Wirral, where Simon has a plan to turn a chunky chain into a standard lamp and Henry picks up a 1963 Ford Anglia to get it back to its best.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. As if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In Hertfordshire, Charlie Luxton helps Roz and Mick Abrahams build a compact contemporary home at the bottom of their garden. Mick and Roz are downsizing from a huge house, and designing a home half the size causes numerous headaches. Charlie shares some bright ideas to demonstrate that, even with a smaller home, it is still possible to think big.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Miners Arms in Nenthead, Cumbria, where sisters Alison Baker and Lynda Clark hope to impress guests with their eco credentials.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and James Widdas run the Rose and Crown in Edgworth, near Bolton, and hope to provide boutique on a budget, but veterans Alison and Lynda are quick to find fault.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nigel and Pok U Regan hope to impress their fellow hoteliers with their east-meets-west atmosphere at Bingham Townhouse, a B&B and Cantonese restaurant in Nottinghamshire.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Float by Boat near Rugby, partners Kev Argent and Tor Johnson run mindfulness breaks from their narrowboat, but Alison and Lynda are not keen on the tiny shared bathroom.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day in the competition sees Andrew and James Widdas from the Rose and Crown confront their critics about their polyester bedding. Nigel and Pok U from the Bingham Townhouse have some explaining to do when it comes to their breakfast service, while Kev and Tor from Float by Boat are left in tears by the payments they receive.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Copper Beech House in Malvern, Worcestershire, a B&B run by ex-professional rugby union player Steve Lloyd and his partner Kathy Leather.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offer recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, revealing how to make the most of kitchen staples and how to be creative with whatever ingredients are available. From ingenious ideas on what to do with forgotten frozen food, to recipes drawn entirely from the store cupboard, Jamie shows how to make frugal nutritious and delicious food.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress.(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company opens its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on special guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-seven-year-old Robert arrives at St George's Hospital, London after colliding with a car on his motorbike. Scans show he has a bleed on the brain, a broken jaw, and two missing teeth, and he faces potentially life-changing injuries and extensive surgery. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mary collapsed in the street on the way to work and hit her head. Doctors need to establish whether it is simple fainting, or whether something more sinister is to blame, with tests revealing she will need a pacemaker fitted. Plus, 27-year-old Jessica comes to A&E with a wound that will not heal, after having a lumpectomy to remove breast cancer tissue two months ago.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Key striker Father Jack drinks himself into oblivion just as the Craggy Island clerics are about to play a vital five-a-side football match against their Rugged Island rivals, led by devious Dick Byrne. Dermot Morgan stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.sub-title: Kicking Bishop Brennan Up the A***
programme.desc: Cheating in the football match backfires on Ted, prompting his Rugged Island rivals to think up a devilish forfeit - and leaving the priest trying to contrive a way of kicking Bishop Brennan's backside and getting away with it. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on special guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company opens its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Ecuador to investigate claims that bananas could one day be used in the fight against a number of viruses including HIV and influenza. Kate Quilton goes in search of a meat substitute that delivers its promise on the tastebuds, visiting the Netherlands where a product called 'plant meat' has been developed by scientists. Plus, Matt Tebbutt visits one of Britain's largest crisp manufacturers to witness the high-tech machinery used to maintain standards of quality.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps to bring a touch of romance and sophistication to Jannine and Shaun Cartwright's neglected bedroom.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Diane Rollisson and Robert Somerville want a holiday home, but can't agree on whether it should be in Northumberland or the Midi-Pyrenees. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple places in both locations to help them decide.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is in Hay on Wye and Bedford, where Henry restores a bespoke workbench, and Simon ponders the possibilities of some discarded planks of wood.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In West Sussex, Charlie Luxton helps Cathryn and Adam Raybould to build their dream home on Cathryn's family farm. The couple like period houses and hope to give their new build a traditional look. The project has a bumpy start and the whole family must muck in if they're going to finish the job.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the White Lodge Hotel in Filey, North Yorkshire, where hosts James and Kim Hodgson hope their love of high standards will win over their guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Amanda Lucia-Charley and her friend Alan Talbot invite the other hoteliers to sample the delights of Crystals on The Prom in Blackpool, where Alan introduces his alter-ego, Ada.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Old Red Lion Country Inn in Upper Poppleton, North Yorkshire, where pair Mark and Jean Lee hope their plain and simple pub B&B will impress their guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest ends at glamping site Camp Katur in Kirklington, North Yorkshire, where Kerry Roy and Nicola Wilding hope to wow their guests with their unique 'geodomes' and see-through pods.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the contest arrives, and while Amanda Lucia-Charley and Alan Talbot receive harsh criticism of their breakfast, Mark and Jean Lee's decor receives scathing reviews.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the pet-friendly Dog in a Doublet in Peterborough, where Della McGinn's passion for animals comes a close second to husband John's passion for food.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offer recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, revealing how to make the most of kitchen staples and how to be creative with whatever ingredients are available.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows software executive Andy and garden designer Nicki, who are planning to build a 'floating' house on the Thames, which would be the first of its kind in the UK. Their architect has come up with an ambitious, experimental design that would see the whole property magically rise up with the flood waters. However, no one knows whether it will work, and the project soon becomes bogged down in the logistical challenges of bringing to fruition such a complex structure on a small island that is only accessible by a narrow footbridge.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A nine-year-old boy falls over and lands on his head, and is left unable to move his limbs. As he waits for the results, the boy's father shares his feelings on the challenges of parenthood. Elsewhere, an 80-year-old woman is brought in with a dangerously swollen tongue and a nine-year-old girl is treated after falling off a swing, leaving consultant Rahail considering how the balance of care between parents and children shifts over time.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

Laura Hamilton and Adrian Simpson help them to resolve the dilemma.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Cholmeley Arms in Burton-le-Coggles, Lincolnshire, where chef John Berry and his wife Lesley hold a sausage-making competition for their guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Islington Mill B&B in Salford, where Joe Spencer and Louise Woodcock have an alternative hosting style as their drag alter egos.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third host Peter Morris welcomes guests to the Pebbles B&B in Seaton, Devon, but despite his breakfast award, not all of the guests are impressed by his culinary offerings.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Tarr Farm Inn on Exmoor, where Hilary Lester and Stacey Wade are proud of their five-star standards. However, Peter is on the warpath after his feedback.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the last day of the competition and there is tension in the air, as Cholmeley Arms hosts John and Lesley want to get to the bottom of Peter's comments.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, revealing how to make the most of kitchen staples and how to be creative with whatever ingredients are available. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree return to help more British people making French castles their homes or running businesses from them. DIY fanatic Fiona contends with sub-zero temperatures as she sets out to renovate a chateau bedroom for her daughter, who is due to visit in two months, while new owners Erin and JB aim to transform the first floor of their property's south wing.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a memorable restoration project in the remote Creuse region of central France. Back in 2003, when Denise Daniel and Doug Ibbs gave up everything in the UK to start a new life abroad, they couldn't have known what was ahead of them. Falling in love with a dilapidated manor house with just four crumbling walls and half a roof, they embarked on a mission to transform it - working day and night, doing everything themselves, year after year - until slowly but surely they created a magnificent edifice out of the ruins they bought off the internet. On the 70th anniversary of the destruction of the original 19th-century building by the German army, the house finally took its finished form.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Mike and Megan, who are building a modernist house on a modest footprint in Suffolk Park, New South Wales.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-nine-year-old Charulata is rushed to A&E after collapsing at home with signs of a stroke. She has a history of strokes, diabetes, and is awaiting a heart bypass, so doctors send her for a CT scan to find out what has happened. Twenty-eight-year-old carpenter Luke - who has epilepsy - arrives after cutting his finger at work. Although his girlfriend Stacy says it looks like a paper cut, he is worried it may trigger a seizure. Meanwhile, 75-year-old Anne comes to St George's after falling and hurting her arm on holiday.(n)

programme.desc: The workshop struggles to convert three quad bikes into frontline police vehicles, while an officer has to crash a pursuit car to protect the public. Steve wants to share his home-made birthday cake - if only he can persuade someone to eat it.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond is dropped into the remote, frozen wastes of Canada to test a watch with a built-in emergency beacon. The distress signal is sent directly to Jeremy Clarkson and James May, who get their hands on two machines and set off on an epic adventure to find their colleague. Plus, Jeremy puts the new 471bhp Lexus RC F coupe thought its paces, while The Fall and The X-Files star Gillian Anderson takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear: Winter Blunderland
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May present their best winter challenges, including playing ice hockey with Suzukis and what occurs if you ski-jump a rocket-powered Mini.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to a series of celebrity guests, ultimately deciding who wins. This time, Mark Watson tries to open an unlocked briefcase.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenters Alex Jones and Rick Edwards, comedian Greg Davies and performance poet John Cooper Clarke join forces with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show in which participants attempt to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: Insert Name Here
programme.desc: Comedians Nish Kumar and Katherine Ryan, historian Kate Williams and sports presenter Gabby Logan join host Sue Perkins and captains Richard Osman and Josh Widdicombe as the comedy quiz returns. The two teams answer questions about famous people who share a name, with the winning trio getting to decide the greatest-ever bearer of this week's moniker.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the comedy panel game, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for interesting - but not necessarily correct - answers. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are guests Aisling Bea, Lee Mack and Jason Manford.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Richardsons
programme.desc: Comedy series starring real-life married couple Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont as they offer a fictional view of their life together. Jon is given career advice from his comedy hero, Jack Dee. Meanwhile, Lucy signs Jon up to be the celebrity judge at the World Dock Pudding Competition in Hebden Bridge.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Mark Watson and Nish Kumar make toast together and Sally Phillips spreads absinthe on it. Greg Davies dishes out the absurd A-list challenges once more.(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain's Go 8 Bit
programme.desc: Simon Gregson and Jayde Adams play a series of video games, including Super Mario Bros, Call Of Duty Advanced Warfare, and Rock Band.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: After the death of Cardinal Wolsey in 1530, Thomas Cromwell became Henry VIII's 'lap dog'. In order to do the king's bidding, he established a network of spies that steered Henry through his troubles with Rome, divorces, assassinations attempts and the establishment of a new religion. as this documentary reveals, though cunning and ambitious, none of these achievements proved sufficient to preserve his life.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston talks to author Joy Hancox about the alchemical roots of London, the design of famous buildings such as The Globe theatre and the mysterious figure of English poet John Byrom. He also meets a modern day potion-maker.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Series exploring ruined engineering projects that have been put to alternative use, including the site of the Chernobyl disaster and a Nazi holiday complex.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage.(n)

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: The historical importance of freight trains, including four-mile long vehicles transporting enough iron ore to build two Eiffel towers.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Colin Salmon narrates a documentary exploring how the lives of humans and animals across a vast area of the planet are affected by the transforming power of the monsoon rains. The end of the dry season in northern Australia sees 300,000 flying foxes gathering to drink while running a gauntlet of freshwater crocodiles, while in Sumatra a female orang-utan guides her youngster to where she knows a fig tree fruits at this time of year. At the Delhi stock exchange, the price of rice escalates every day the rains fail to materialise and priests submerge themselves in water and beseech Varuna, god of water, to come.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: A look at how the 20,000 islands in the region became colonised by humans and animals. For the Polynesians, the Pacific's first people, their journeys constituted a feat of navigational ingenuity, while animals often had to rely on violent forces of nature, such as cyclones and tsunamis, to reach the shores.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring ship graveyards, including Scapa Flow in Orkney, where German warships were scuttled after the First World War, and Mallows Bay, home to 200 wrecks colonised by wildlife.(n)

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The staff await an important royal visit, but first Stan has to pick just the right toupee, and Bren is determined to get the tables cleared away in time. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow.(n)

programme.desc: Bren's mother wreaks havoc in the factory and incurs Stan's wrath by pursuing an ill-considered romance, while Anita organises a heated debate on the workers' current situation. Comedy, with Victoria Wood and Julie Walters.(n)

programme.desc: The effect of the rainy season on wildlife in Asia, with floods forcing animals to flee from Kaziranga National Park in north-east India in search of the safety of the hills. In Cambodia, the Mekong river swells so much that water is forced back up one of its tributaries to fill the vast Tonle Sap lake - one of the most productive freshwater fisheries on Earth. Fresh grass draws nomads and their vast herds of livestock back to their homelands, but they are stalked by hungry wolves and hyenas, which move in at night for the kill. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.desc: The other side of the monsoon, as the winds bring dry air and drought. Budgerigars swarm in the Australian desert, gathering at a precious waterhole, and although birds of prey try to ambush them, they are mesmerised by the array of bright colours on show. On the island of Borneo, a bearded pig hunts for seafood on the beach, while in the Rajasthan desert, nomads haggle over the price of camels, which can provide vital transport during this lean season. The mugger crocodiles of Sri Lanka stalk the animals taking a much-needed drink and a young leopard learning to hunt discovers there is no such thing as an easy meal. Colin Salmon narrates.(n)

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How the price of bread is set, the production of speedy lawnmowers, and how coconuts are employed to clean water.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests. With the first guests of the season about to turn up, the pool continues to be a problematic nightmare for Mandy and Steve. Dick's wife Angel shows some clever, cheap, wedding tips - but will Dick and the kids like them?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help Cambridgeshire couple Wendy and Pete, who cannot decide whether they want to move to Great Yarmouth in Norfolk or Almeria in Spain.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp's weekly project is to design and make a name sign for her son's wall, and she shows how to make a cushion out of a jumper and tries her hand at Mexican tin art.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Church Street Cobbles in Macclesfield, Cheshire, where foodie Debbie Quinn and housekeeper and cook Isobel Reisen recently opened a B&B above a barber's.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Peterborough, 39-year-old land surveyor and part-time musician Muneer hopes to win over his guests with a menu based on Pakistani recipes passed down through his family for generations. The authenticity extends beyond his food, with Muneer seating his guests on the floor of his specially dressed dining room. His guests include driving instructor Christine, hairdresser Ben, fiery Brazilian Rosie and fridge salesman Gregory. While his food receives mixed reviews, His guests discover his master plan for musical success, suffer bad jokes and find out that one of their fellow diners believes in mermaids.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackpool compete for the £1,000 prize, beginning with practising Buddhist Antonio and former redcoat Jason, who serve up a sushi starter. A discovery on day two at the home of fireman Daz and his wife Kim raises a few eyebrows, while the final night sees foodies Martin and Kate prepare an authentic Thai menu and a chocolate dessert.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra grudgingly agrees to let Ray have more involvement in the daily decision-making process, giving him the freedom to shop for grocery items he likes and choose where the family will go on holiday. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra, Ray and Amy get trapped at Frank and Marie's house during a snowstorm, but they actually find themselves having a good time - until an unguarded comment alters the mood. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Monica Horan.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: And the Dish Ran Away with the Spoon
programme.desc: Double-length episode. Donny issues a lawsuit against Daphne for jilting him at the altar, and Mel plots revenge against Niles after he abandoned his marriage to her. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce, Jane Leeves and Saul Rubinek.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Iain from south Wales are looking to buy a holiday home in Ibiza after they fell in love with the island on a family holiday six years ago. They're especially drawn to the pretty town of Santa Eularia and have set their hearts on somewhere with a sea view, so call upon the skills of property expert Ben Hillman to find them the home of their dreams for their £275,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Alan and Sandra from Carlisle search for a retirement retreat in Alicante. However, traffic enthusiast Alan has an unusual request - he wants the new home to be near a busy road.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp revisit house-hunters in Norfolk, first featured in January 2014. Young-at-heart pensioners Jon and Peggy were relocating from Derby to fulfil their dream of living by the sea - and with £450,000 to spend, they had an exacting list of criteria that had to be met. Meanwhile, Yvonne and Lisa from Leeds had reached the end of their tether after spending more than a year searching for a home in a rural district. A year on, how are both pairs getting on?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: The comedian fights the good fight on behalf of the British public, tackling viewers' consumer problems, no matter how big or small. In this edition, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing helps Joe launch the UK's first ever consumer rights rave, while the show's fearless assistant Mark Silcox tests the slogan of a high street bank. Joe also infiltrates hackable smart tech and launches a musical raid on the offices of the world's largest online retailer. Plus, comedian Lucy Beaumont spies on CCTV itself.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: It is the third phase of the selection process and the celebrity recruits are put in specially selected pairs to test their ability to work as a unit. Each partnership must eat, sleep and operate as a duo as they are put through brutal tasks, including a paired abseil down a 60-metre cliff. The course front runners - reality TV Star Joey Essex and Paralympian Lauren Steadman - discover that although they may shine as individuals, it's unity that matters, and their lack of teamwork means they're both at risk of failure.(n)

programme.title: Mo Gilligan's All Star Happy Hour
programme.desc: The stand-up presents a mix of comedy, music and games, with members of the public going head to head with famous faces in a quest to win prizes. Accompanied by some surprising guests, Mo also gives his take on the week's TV and funniest clips, with some of the nation's favourite music acts performing their biggest hits in a new way.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill, Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp, actor and writer Roisin Conaty, and broadcaster Bill Turnbull take part in the charity contest. Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted challenges, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Grayson Perry continues his mission to bring the nation together through art. From his studio, he talks to other famous artists, creatives and celebrities about the artworks they are making during their time in isolation and hosts masterclasses to teach viewers new creative skills. This week he asks people to send in their artworks of animals - whether it be a beloved pet, an abstract or an exotic creature. At the end of the isolation period, the Turner Prize-winning artist plans to curate an exhibition chronicling the changing moods of Britain throughout this unique time.(n)

However, with everyone dreading Christine's meat-free and bland-sounding menu, Rosie may not be the only one who has to bite her lip.(n)

programme.desc: Ray discovers the leader of local group the Frontier Girls is intending to sell a record number of cookies in his area, and sets out to beat her at her own game. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Amy Aquino.(n)

programme.desc: Robert is ecstatic when he is recommended for a job with the FBI, until it starts to look like Marie's intrusive ways may scupper his chances.(n)

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with two pairs of house-hunters. Requiring three bedrooms and space for three cars, as well as their dog, Jodie and Andy finally found what they were looking for in Portsmouth, and although the negotiations didn't work out, that wasn't the end of their story. In Stansted, Phil finds out whether Jocelyn and Simon's dream of a rural idyll was ever completely satisfied.(n)

programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits.(n)

The course front runners - reality TV Star Joey Essex and Paralympian Lauren Steadman - discover that although they may shine as individuals, it's unity that matters, and their lack of teamwork means they're both at risk of failure.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Phil Wang take on guest captain Alan Carr and Harriet Kemsley. David O'Doherty and Joe Wilkinson join wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.(n)

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: The baker visits one of the world's smallest restaurants in Hiroshima, where he gets a memorable cookery lesson from a survivor of the atomic bomb, and attends a rice festival in the snowy north of the country where local people fight with six-metre-long bamboo sticks to determine if they will get a good harvest. He also samples the buffet on a bullet train, discovers why Okinawa is embracing American fast food, and visits the ancient capital of Kyoto, where he attempts to develop a taste for tofu.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: The actor and writer celebrates everything that is keeping the Great British public entertained at home. Guests include comedians Tim Vine, Mo Gilligan, Harry Hill and Rosie Jones, classical singer Russell Watson, Love Island's Megan Barton Hanson, radio host Roman Kemp, TV presenter Steph McGovern, newsreader Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Made in Chelsea star Jamie Laing, singer Suggs, and more members of the public.(n)

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam and Jonny finally bring their 'females' home to meet their parents, and just to add to the potential confusion both girls are called Lucy. Before the objects of their affection can make it out of the car, the boys rush into the house to give Mum and Dad a last-minute rundown of the 'rules'. Though Mum seems delighted, Dad fails to make the best first impression. Nevertheless, the evening is saved from complete disaster thanks to some exciting revelations. Comedy, starring Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Paul Ritter.(n)

programme.title: Spring at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: In any other year, as spring arrives on Jimmy Doherty's farm, his 280-acre park would be teeming with families, eager to see his collection of exotic animals. But 2020 is no ordinary year, and while there are no guests, his animals still need caring for. This documentary, filmed by a small crew, offers a unique insight into how Jimmy and his team are managing. In the first episode, lambs, goats and reindeer are all due to give birth, a camel needs to find its mate and Jimmy builds hides and nesting boxes to keep an eye on what the badgers, birds and fish are getting up to.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Compilation of highlights from the past week's programmes, including tips on making light boxes, paper flowers, 3D models, origami and a name sign for a child's room. Plus, memorable moments from Kirstie's other series and ideas for craft projects sent in by viewers.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Donna McDougal's search for the armour in the lake continues. Tim and Krys, with the help of Tim's brother Ben, continue in their struggle to get the attic converted in time for the first guests of the season. Dick and his mother-in-law Jenny struggle with preparing and freezing 227kg of pork, before attempting to turn the final 2kg into salamis.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Chesterfield couple Joy and John, who are searching for their dream holiday home around the bustling town of Fuengirola on Spain's Costa del Sol.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp makes a 3D letter and decoupages it with an old map, topped off with an origami kite. She also has fun experimenting with string art to create a hot-air balloon and raids her button box to transform an old lampshade. Plus, Kirstie goes online to speak to crafting experts and calls on the crafting community to send in the things they're making during lockdown.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Cranberries Hideaway in Kilmington, Devon, where owner Steve and his partner Verity hope the luxury touches will win over their guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, mobile hairdresser Ben plans a gourmet night. Most of his guests are looking forward to some meat after Christine's vegetarian feast the night before. And after laid-back Muneer and critical Rosie seem to have put their tension behind them at Christine's, Ben's determined to take things up a notch as he entertains his guests with his amazing body art, cooking skills and pet crabs.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fifty-somethings Lesley and Roger, music teachers Emma and Chris, and teacher and ex-soldier Bonita and Tony take part in the culinary challenge in Leicester. Highlights include an international-themed evening inspired by the couple's love of luxury cruises and the youngest contestants desperate to prove they can serve up fine cuisine.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra amuse the guests at a party by doing a wickedly accurate impression of Frank and Marie - who are not amused and respond in kind with an impersonation of their own.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finds a cassette that contains a message from Ray's college girlfriend, and wants to know why he has saved it for all these years. She also shows Ray some of her keepsakes from previous relationships, prompting a severe case of jealousy on his part.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles is forced to pretend he is Mel's husband at a party, and Frasier hires a butler who spoils everyone in the household. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio shrink begins to date the attractive attorney who's defending him against Donny - who was jilted at the altar by Daphne - although he suspects that she is unfairly running up his bill.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

In Alicante, Sophie Rodriguez has a challenge on her hands as she seeks a three-bed holiday home near the sea for Fife couple Adrian and Marsha.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.sub-title: Lake District and Scottish Highlands Revisits
programme.desc: House-hunting show with Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer. The property experts catch up with two couples who hoped to realise dreams of finding a new home and business. Catherine and Mark wanted to move from the Midlands to the Lake District with plans to set up a hot-tub company, while Christine and Keith gave up life in Cambridgeshire to go in search of a hotel to run in the Highlands.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef heads to the Seascape restaurant in the village of Islip on Long Island, New York, in a bid to change the fortunes of its owner, who is nearly $1million in debt. The subsequent disappearance of a second youngster leads to a race against time to save her from a similar fate. Gritty drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The rape of a woman in a coma leads the team to question a doctor whose stem cell research is funded by a billionaire with Parkinson's disease. They discover the tycoon is working in tandem with the medic to find a cure, but the pair's unethical procedures eventually come under scrutiny in court. Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate the rape of a cellist, and initially believe her conductor is to blame for the attack - especially given his penchant for erotic film-making. However, they soon discover an obsessive stalker is watching her from afar - and will stop at nothing to continue his monitoring. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Cabot takes a child abuse case, determined to find evidence that will convict the defendant. Her personal feelings tempt her to bend the rules, leading Stabler and Benson into an illegal search of the victim's home - a move that threatens all of their careers.(n)

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: Chicago firefighter Kelly Severide is accused of a hit-and-run accident, which later turns into a vehicular homicide when one of the victims dies in hospital from her injuries.(n)

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: After the body of a young woman is found with multiple gunshot wounds, Intelligence identify links to other deaths and realise they are dealing with a serial killer. Meanwhile, Lindsay finally meets her biological father.(n)

programme.desc: A private investigator's murder draws Briscoe and Green toward a case of corruption in a baseball league - where the manager will do anything to ensure his team wins. They learn the victim had unearthed a scandal in which an ineligible player was being fielded in every game - and his discovery may have led to his death. Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green look into the shooting of defence lawyer Vance Grodie, who had just won an acquittal for a suspected cop killer. Their investigations reveal a link to a network of conservative extremists, and the discovery leads the case into an examination of civil rights. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baseball star becomes the prime suspect in the murder of his limousine driver, who had been regularly supplying him with steroids. Briscoe and Green's investigation reveals blackmail as the underlying motive for the crime. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An officer is found dead, and evidence initially suggests she committed suicide as a result of a nervous breakdown. However, a sceptical Ducky believes foul play was involved - and the team later discovers the victim was caught up in a love triangle. Kevin Quigley guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates the stabbing of a teacher whose body has been found in a caretaker's cupboard. The hunt for her killer leads to the discovery that she was caught up in a love triangle at school involving a student with multiple identities. Legal drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in the East River leads Briscoe and Green to investigate the drug-addicted brother of a famous footballer - and as the case unfolds, it becomes clear she may not have been the killer's only victim. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green think they have got their man when the prime suspect in a murder investigation knows every detail about the killing. However, when he claims he acquired his information from a psychic vision, the detectives have their work cut out. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. McGee and Gibbs set out to identify the culprits and find a cure for the disease. Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent

programme.desc: The stepdaughter of a judge known for his harsh sentencing of sex offenders is raped and murdered. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate an assault on a restaurateur's wife, and find the attack is similar to a rape committed the previous week. They discover a locksmith visited both women's houses before the incident, but when they examine the stolen goods found in his toolbox, a new connection emerges - putting another suspect in the frame. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: When a known sex offender is found dead and mutilated, Intelligence officers look into a private police force that may be covering up illegal activities. Meanwhile, Halstead runs a DNA check on Jimmy and discovers he is not Erin's father.(n)

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: The investigation of a jogger's brutal murder in a park is blocked by a church offering sanctuary to the prime suspects. Rixton goes behind Voight's back to question a gang member in relation to the case.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A high-school student is killed by a hit-and-run driver, with tyre marks at the scene indicating it was deliberate. The case is complicated when the chief suspect is murdered by his mother. Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The admissions director of an exclusive Manhattan elementary school is bludgeoned to death and Briscoe and Green initially believe she was murdered by a parent determined to have their child enrolled. However, as their investigation continues, a member of the school's staff comes under suspicion. Guest starring Roger Rees, with Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the death of a Russian model, and when the autopsy reveals a host of old injuries they suspect long-term abuse. Further tests are made on the body, and the findings suggest medical malpractice may have been the cause, leading the detectives to track down the person responsible for her death. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star.(n)

programme.desc: The detectives arrest two students suspected of raping and murdering a cancer patient who grew marijuana in her apartment. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to the Dorset seaside resort of Bournemouth to help 67-year-old Ken Robins, the owner of a struggling hotel where all 55 bedrooms have been named after movie stars and famous musicians. After inspecting and experiencing the facilities, Alex reveals she thinks that Ken has not gone far enough with the celebrity theme and offers advice on how to inject more glitz and sparkle into the hotel. Will former accountant Ken agree with her more radical and glamorous blueprint for fun?(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The three finalists take on their last set of challenges before judges Patrick Grant and May Martin decide who wins the title of Britain's best amateur sewer. The first test is to follow a pattern for a man's shirt, a task that drives one contestant to tears, and then the trio have to show off their hand-stitching skills by decorating an evening bag. For the finale they are asked to create a made-to-measure evening dress in a luxury fabric - and are in for a surprise when they see who their models are. Claudia Winkleman presents.(n)

programme.title: Ghost Nation
programme.desc: Jason, Steve and Dave respond to an urgent call in New Jersey, where a horror writer believes there is a poltergeist in his house.(n)

When he later steps in to protect a manservant from Prince Arthur and encounters the Great Dragon, the young sorcerer begins an adventure that will determine his destiny.(n)

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people.(n)

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War.(n)

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: First episode of the drama about a military bomb disposal team working in London during the blitz. New recruit Brian Ash is thrown in at the deep end. Starring Anthony Andrews.(n)

programme.title: Codebreaker
programme.sub-title: Codebreaker: The Secret Genius of World War Two
programme.desc: The first of a two-part docu-drama uncovering the secret of how a 24-year-old maths student and a GPO engineer hacked into Hitler's personal code machine - not the Enigma coding system, but something even more intimidating known as his 'secret writer'. Despite his achievements, codebreaker Alan Turing's life was dogged by tragedy.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Final episode of the drama following a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Warrior Challenge
programme.desc: Action from a UCMMA event, the mixed martial arts promotion that stages bouts at The Troxy.(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Maxine Peake and Rupert Penry-Jones return in the legal drama. Clive has finally become a silk, but his celebration party is dramatically cut short when news comes through that the son of the head of chambers has been arrested for killing a police officer. Martha is assigned to defend the young man - but the evidence against him seems overwhelming. Meanwhile, Billy struggles with a secret and a new practice manager sends ripples through the clerks' room. Neil Stuke, Miranda Raison and Frances Barber co-star.(n)

programme.title: Undercover Hooligan (2016)
programme.desc: Suspended cop Michael Clarke is tasked with infiltrating an infamous London gang and bringing down its leader. British crime drama, starring Kris Johnson and Patrick Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Long Weekend (1978)
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves.(n)

programme.title: Dam Busters: The Race to Smash the German Dams
programme.desc: James Holland presents an analysis of the 1943 air attacks by the RAF, in which 19 Lancaster bombers ventured into the industrial heartland of Germany to destroy three heavily defended dams with the new bouncing bomb. He argues that the raid was the best-executed and most important of all time and explores whether its true impact has been underestimated - and if the result should have been even greater.(n)

programme.title: Codebreaker
programme.sub-title: Codebreaker: The Secret Genius of World War Two
programme.desc: The first of a two-part docu-drama uncovering the secret of how a 24-year-old maths student and a GPO engineer hacked into Hitler's personal code machine - not the Enigma coding system, but something even more intimidating known as his 'secret writer'. programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Elenya (1992)
programme.desc: Aa 12-year-old half-Italian Welsh schoolgirl stumbles across a wounded German airman during the Second World War and secretly cares for him - only for tragedy to strike as the local townsfolk discover what she has been up to. Drama, starring Pascale Delafourge Jones, Klaus Behrendt, Margaret John and Sue Jones Davies.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny visits Patrick and Vanessa from Aberdare in South Wales, who have re-mortgaged their home and bought two dilapidated cottages they have not even seen for £25,000.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash employs some new technology that does not work. A new recruit gets some training, and a magazine article causes tension amid the team's superior officers.(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Series recreating the court cases of three of the Second World War's most notorious Nazis, using eyewitness interviews and archive footage, as well as considering the interviews they underwent at the hands of lawyers and psychologists. The first episode focuses on the trial of Albert Speer, Hitler's chief architect and armaments minister, regarded by many as a 'good' Nazi. Nathaniel Parker stars.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals.(n)

programme.title: Active Measures (2018)
programme.desc: Director Jack Bryan addresses the allegations of collusion between the Trump campaign and agents of the Russian state.(n)

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo.(n)

programme.title: The English Teacher (2013)
programme.desc: A teacher is reunited with her favourite past pupil, who has returned from New York having failed to succeed as a playwright and under pressure from his family to get a job. She puts her reputation on the line to prove her protege has talent by getting the school to put on a production of his play. Comedy drama, starring Julianne Moore, Michael Angarano and Greg Kinnear.(n)

Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny meets Lisa, who wants to swap her boring IT job for property development. Having bought a small three-bedroom house in Macclesfield, she sets about transforming it from a bland beige box to something that, thanks to her unique design ideas, will really stand out from the crowd. But are rose petal and leaf skeletons stuck to the wall really going to appeal to potential buyers?(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash and Machin must dispose of a bomb in a school - only to find another device close by, forcing them to split up and defuse one each.(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive.(n)

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: An insight into the crimes of the Peaky Blinders, the Birmingham-based gang that eventually forged ties with the Krays as part of the flourishing gang culture of the 1960s.(n)

programme.title: London Real
programme.desc: Broadcaster and entrepreneur Brian Rose is in debate with some of the world's most interesting minds. Tonight, he talks to Yogi and author Sadhguru.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts another celebrity edition of the classic game show, in which he invites well-known faces to team up with their relatives in a bid to win a prize of up to £30,000 for charity. Actor Nicola Stapleton joins her clan to play for Cancer Research, while comedian Johnny Vegas and his family aim to win big for the Willowbrook Hospice.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong quizzes a line-up of contestants who work on shows with a wildlife or natural history theme. They are birdwatcher Bill Oddie and historian Tessa Dunlop, Countryfile presenters John Craven and Shauna Lowry, TV gardener Diarmuid Gavin and Coast presenter Miranda Krestovnikoff, and Blue Peter hosts-turned-wildlife presenters Helen Skelton and Radzi Chinyanganya. Richard Osman is the man with the answers.(n)

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay invites another pair of famous faces to team up with their relatives and play the classic game show, in a bid to win a prize of up to £30,000 for charity. programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Members of the BBC's news team Bill Turnbull, Sian Williams, Nick Robinson, Louise Minchin and Charlie Stayt, challenge the general knowledge champions in a bid to win a cash prize for their nominated charity. Dermot Murnaghan presents.(n)

programme.title: Weakest Link

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present the general knowledge quiz. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another star-studded round of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz, this time with a 'British Film' theme. The celebrities aiming to come up with the obscure answers are Quadrophenia stars Phil Daniels and Leslie Ash, Trainspotting and Shallow Grave actor Keith Allen, Gregory's Girl actress Clare Grogan, Mean Machine's Vas Blackwood, This is England's Thomas Turgoose, comedian, impressionist and screenwriter Phil Cornwell and Sally Thomsett, who played the youngest Waterbury sibling, Phyllis, in the 1970 adaptation of The Railway Children.(n)
